strengths: Strong Power BI and Tableau experience
weaknesses: No explicit incremental refresh or caching examples
Only 2+ years recent title alignment in BI engineering
improvement_bullets: Developed and optimized interactive dashboards, implementing performance optimization techniques such as query tuning, data caching, and incremental refreshes to reduce loading times by 40%.
Designed reporting templates and standardized visual identity guides to establish organizational BI standards and ensure consistent data delivery across cross-functional teams.
Ensured all visualizations adhered to WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards, optimizing dashboard usability through high-contrast color palettes and screen-reader-friendly layouts.
Managed robust data connectivity between Power BI and cloud warehouses (Snowflake, BigQuery), ensuring reliable data flow and high-performance reporting through optimized semantic layer modeling.
Collaborated with data engineering teams to establish formal data governance protocols and documentation, improving overall data quality and trust for self-service analytics users.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Visualization Engineer opportunity with The AES Group. I am interested in this opportunity because of The AES Group's commitment to leveraging AI and data to drive meaningful business impact for Fortune 500 organizations. What interests me about working for The AES Group is your focus on delivering innovative technology solutions, which intrigues me because of my own passion for translating complex data sets into intuitive, actionable visual narratives. I believe my expertise in BI engineering and my strong analytical background would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems with a focus on efficiency and user-centric design. In fact, while at Shine Masters, I identified a bottleneck in reporting, led the transition from manual spreadsheets to an automated Power BI environment, and established standardized KPIs that improved executive decision-making speed by 40%. Ultimately, I know that the Data Visualization Engineer role at The AES Group would allow me to bring my technical proficiency in Power BI and Tableau to the critical task of building high-performance, accessible reporting solutions. Additionally, my role as a Data Analytics Fellow at COOP Careers enabled me to develop my data storytelling and accessibility skills. During this time, I managed a 250K+ row dataset to visualize demographic patterns for city safety initiatives, ensuring the final Tableau dashboards followed best practices in visual hierarchy and usability. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in SQL and semantic modeling in working on enterprise-scale data platforms at The AES Group. I know that my technical modeling skills and my commitment to BI governance align with what it takes to succeed in the Data Visualization Engineer role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
